page.settings.title: Settings
toast.loading.title: Loading
param.server.ctx_size.name: Context window size
param.server.ctx_size.desc: Set the context window size available to the model (tokens). The context must hold both the prompt and subsequently generated tokens. Larger values improve long chat/document capability, but KV cache RAM/VRAM usage grows accordingly, and prefill/decode compute may also increase. Set to 0 to read the default context size from the model file metadata.
param.server.flash_attn.name: Flash Attention
param.server.flash_attn.desc: Control whether to enable Flash Attention (fused attention optimization): on = force enable, off = force disable, auto = auto-detect backend capability. Enabling usually reduces attention memory-bandwidth overhead and improves throughput (especially with long contexts or large-batch prefill), and may also reduce VRAM usage; but on some hardware/driver/backend combinations it can regress or be unstable. If you see abnormal speed, unusually high VRAM peaks, or need A/B testing, switch to off; when unsure, use auto.
param.server.no_mmap.name: Disable mmap loading
param.server.no_mmap.desc: When enabled, the model is loaded without memory mapping (mmap) and weights are read into memory normally. mmap usually reduces copying at startup and lets the OS page data on demand, resulting in faster startup and better memory behavior. Disabling mmap can avoid mapping failures, SIGBUS, or stalls in some file systems / network drives / permission-limited / container environments, but often increases one-time memory usage and load time. Toggle this option when loading is slow, fails, or is unstable to find a more stable/faster combination.
param.server.mlock.name: Lock model in RAM
param.server.mlock.desc: Lock model weights in physical memory (RAM) to reduce the risk of occasional slowdowns during inference caused by swapping/compression, improving latency stability. This can significantly increase required RAM and may require elevated permissions/quotas on some systems; if memory is insufficient it may prevent startup or cause system memory pressure.
param.server.embedding.name: Embedding mode
param.server.embedding.desc: Enable embeddings, which encodes input text into vector representations for semantic search, clustering, similarity matching, and RAG. This mode is typically used with embedding-specific models or models that support embedding outputs. Unlike text generation, it focuses on vector output and has different throughput/memory characteristics. Enable only when you need an embeddings API or vector output.
param.server.rerank.name: Rerank mode
param.server.rerank.desc: Enable reranking, which scores the relevance of 'query + candidate documents/passages' and sorts them by score. Commonly used as a rerank stage in RAG to improve citation quality. This mode is typically intended for rerank-specific models (often cross-encoder style) and follows a different inference path than standard generation models. It adds scoring compute overhead but can significantly improve ranking quality. Enable only when you need a rerank API/feature.
param.server.temp.name: Temperature
param.server.temp.desc: Controls sampling randomness. Lower values produce more deterministic output, closer to high-probability tokens (more conservative, less diverse but possibly more rigid); higher values flatten the distribution for more diverse/creative output but with higher risk of drifting or unreliable content. Common range is about 0.1–1.2; setting 0 typically approaches greedy/deterministic decoding (behavior depends on implementation).
param.server.top_p.name: Top-P (nucleus sampling)
param.server.top_p.desc: Nucleus sampling (Top-P): at each step, sample only from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability reaches P. Smaller P narrows candidates and stabilizes output; larger P increases variety. P = 1.0 disables Top-P truncation.
param.server.top_k.name: Top-K
param.server.top_k.desc: Top-K sampling: at each step, sample only from the K most probable tokens. Smaller K is more conservative; larger K is more diverse. Too small can lead to repetitive phrasing; too large can increase drift. K = 0 disables Top-K truncation.
param.server.min_p.name: Min-P
param.server.min_p.desc: Minimum probability threshold (Min-P): keep only tokens whose probability is at least (probability of the most likely token × P). This filters out extremely low-probability tail tokens to reduce gibberish/garbage tokens or extreme jumps. Larger P filters more aggressively and stabilizes output but can become overly conservative. P = 0.0 disables Min-P.
param.server.presence_penalty.name: Presence penalty
param.server.presence_penalty.desc: Apply a penalty to any token that has already appeared in the current context to encourage introducing new words/new information and reduce repeated mentions. Higher values avoid repetition and topic loops more strongly, but too high can cause erratic wording or off-topic output. 0.0 disables.
param.server.repeat_penalty.name: Repeat penalty
param.server.repeat_penalty.desc: Repeat penalty multiplier: reduces the probability of sampling tokens that have appeared recently to suppress 'looping' behavior. 1.0 means no penalty; values above 1.0 increase suppression (commonly 1.05–1.2). Too high can harm fluency or force unnatural wording.
param.server.frequency_penalty.name: Frequency penalty
param.server.frequency_penalty.desc: Apply a penalty based on how often a token appears in the current context: more frequent tokens get stronger penalties to reduce high-frequency repetition and template-like phrasing. Unlike presence penalty, it accumulates with count. Higher values suppress repetition more, but too high can hurt coherence. 0.0 disables.
param.server.batch_size.name: Batch size
param.server.batch_size.desc: Logical batch size: mainly affects how many tokens can be processed in parallel during the prompt prefill stage. Increasing it often improves throughput and speeds up long prompts, but increases peak VRAM/RAM usage and can trigger OOM or performance regressions on some backends. This value is more like a logical ceiling; actual execution also depends on micro-batch size, backend implementation, and available VRAM.
param.server.ubatch_size.name: Micro-batch size (uBatch)
param.server.ubatch_size.desc: Physical micro-batch size: splits a large logical batch into smaller micro-batches to balance throughput and peak VRAM usage. Larger values reduce splitting overhead and speed up prefill but increase VRAM peaks; smaller values are more VRAM-friendly and stable but may reduce throughput. When VRAM is tight or concurrency is high, lowering this value can help avoid OOM.
param.server.parallel.name: Parallel slots
param.server.parallel.desc: Number of parallel slots the server can handle concurrently, effectively limiting concurrent sessions. Higher values increase concurrency, but each slot consumes KV cache and runtime resources, increasing RAM/VRAM usage and potentially reducing per-request throughput or increasing latency. -1 means auto (typically based on context size and available resources). Increase for short contexts/smaller models with high concurrency needs; be conservative for long contexts or VRAM-constrained setups.
param.server.cache_ram.name: KV cache memory limit
param.server.cache_ram.desc: Maximum KV cache RAM/VRAM budget (MiB). KV cache stores attention K/V states and is a primary memory consumer for long contexts and high concurrency. Higher limits support longer contexts or more slots, but increase memory usage. -1 means no limit. 0 disables KV cache (usually makes autoregressive generation unusable or extremely slow; only for special tests).
param.server.kv_unified.name: Unified KV buffer
param.server.kv_unified.desc: Use a unified/shared KV buffer management strategy across sequences/sessions to reuse and allocate KV memory more efficiently under concurrency. This often reduces fragmentation and peak usage and improves stability, but in extreme cases can affect throughput or memory layout behavior. When slots (parallel) are in auto mode, this is often enabled by default for more stable resource management.
param.server.cache_type_k.name: KV cache type (K)
param.server.cache_type_k.desc: Data type (precision/quantization) for K vectors in the KV cache. Higher precision (e.g., f32) uses more VRAM and is usually more stable. Lower precision or quantization (e.g., q8/q5/q4/iq4) can significantly reduce VRAM usage and may improve speed in bandwidth-bound scenarios, but can introduce minor quality changes or numerical stability differences; support varies by backend. Options: f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl, q5_0, q5_1.
param.server.cache_type_v.name: KV cache type (V)
param.server.cache_type_v.desc: Data type (precision/quantization) for V vectors in the KV cache. Similar to K type, it mainly affects KV cache VRAM usage and bandwidth. Lower precision/quantization can reduce memory usage and increase supported context length or concurrency, but may cause minor quality changes or instability in rare environments. Try lower precision when VRAM is tight or you need longer context/higher concurrency, and compare results. Options: f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl, q5_0, q5_1.
param.server.threads.name: Threads
param.server.threads.desc: Number of CPU threads used during inference (affects CPU compute and scheduling in some backends). More threads is not always faster: too many can cause contention, cache thrashing, or resource starvation, increasing latency. -1 means auto-detect (typically based on physical cores or system recommendations). For CPU inference or stable latency, test around the number of physical cores to find the best value.
param.server.seed.name: Random seed
param.server.seed.desc: Random number generator seed for sampling reproducibility: with the same model, prompt, and sampling parameters, a fixed seed usually yields identical or highly similar outputs, useful for regression tests and reproducing issues. -1 uses a random seed per start/request for more diversity.
param.server.directio.name: Direct I/O
param.server.directio.desc: Enable Direct I/O, which affects model loading speed. Enabled by default. llama.cpp's implementation may be problematic; if you encounter loading issues, disable it.
param.server.no_jinja.name: Disable Jinja
param.server.no_jinja.desc: Disable the Jinja template engine for chat. Jinja is enabled by default, so this option is false by default.
param.server.chat_template.name: Built-in chat template
param.server.chat_template.desc: Generally do not use this unless your GGUF model does not include a built-in chat template. If you do use it, set 'Disable Jinja' to 1; otherwise, current llama.cpp will not apply the built-in template. Default is empty, meaning use the model's built-in template. If the built-in template is inaccurate, try overriding it here.
param.server.context_shift.name: Context shift
param.server.context_shift.desc: Disabled by default. Enable the KV cache sliding-window mechanism (also called 'context shift' or 'context compression'). When the context exceeds --ctx-size, the system automatically discards the oldest tokens (from the left) and keeps the most recent tokens, instead of failing with an error.
param.server.no_webui.name: Disable webui
param.server.no_webui.desc: Since it's the web UI that comes with llama.cpp, it generally doesn't need to be enabled.
param.server.reasoning_format.name: Reasoning Format
param.server.reasoning_format.desc: controls whether thought tags are allowed and/or extracted from the response, and in which format they're returned; one of: - none: leaves thoughts unparsed in message.content; - deepseek: puts thoughts in message.reasoning_content; - deepseek-legacy: keeps <think> tags in message.content while also populating message.reasoning_content(default: auto)
param.server.reasoning_budget.name: Reasoning Budget
param.server.reasoning_budget.desc: Controls the allowed amount of reasoning; -1 indicates an unlimited reasoning budget, or 0 indicates disabling thinking. Any other value specifies the maximum length of the reasoning chain, though this may not always be fully effective (default: -1).
param.server.no_mmproj_offload.name: no mmproj offload
param.server.no_mmproj_offload.desc: Offload mmproj projection to CPU. You can enable this option when GPU VRAM is insufficient, but CPU image processing performance is typically inferior to that of the GPU.
param.benchmark.repetitions.name: Repetitions
param.benchmark.repetitions.desc: Number of repetitions to obtain more stable performance data. Higher values are more stable but take longer.
param.benchmark.output.name: Output format
param.benchmark.output.desc: Select the output format for benchmark results. The output file will be saved under the benchmarks folder.
param.benchmark.output_err.name: Error output format
param.benchmark.output_err.desc: Select the format for error output. Empty means no error output.
param.benchmark.delay.name: Delay
param.benchmark.delay.desc: Delay between tests (seconds) to cool down the system or avoid resource contention.
param.benchmark.verbose.name: Verbose
param.benchmark.verbose.desc: Enable verbose mode and show more debug information.
param.benchmark.n_prompt.name: Prompt length
param.benchmark.n_prompt.desc: Prompt token count. Supported formats: single value (512), multiple values (512,1024), range (0-4096), range + step (0-4096+512).
param.benchmark.n_gen.name: Generation length
param.benchmark.n_gen.desc: Number of tokens to generate. Multiple values are supported, comma-separated. Example: 128,256.
param.benchmark.pg.name: Prompt + generation
param.benchmark.pg.desc: Set prompt and generation lengths together in prompt,gen format. Example: 512,128.
param.benchmark.n_depth.name: Prefill depth
param.benchmark.n_depth.desc: Prefill depth for testing performance under different KV cache states. 0 starts from scratch; other values reuse partially prefilled cache.
param.benchmark.batch_size.name: Batch size
param.benchmark.batch_size.desc: Logical batch size: affects how many tokens can be processed per prefill step. Multiple values are supported, comma-separated. Example: 128,256,512,1024.
param.benchmark.ubatch_size.name: Micro-batch size
param.benchmark.ubatch_size.desc: Physical micro-batch size: splits a large logical batch into smaller micro-batches. Multiple values are supported, comma-separated. Example: 128,256,512,1024.
param.benchmark.cache_type_k.name: KV cache type (K)
param.benchmark.cache_type_k.desc: Data type for K vectors in the KV cache. Precision affects VRAM usage and performance.
param.benchmark.cache_type_v.name: KV cache type (V)
param.benchmark.cache_type_v.desc: Data type for V vectors in the KV cache. Precision affects VRAM usage and performance.
param.benchmark.threads.name: Threads
param.benchmark.threads.desc: CPU thread count used during inference. Multiple values are supported, comma-separated, e.g. 8 or 4,8,16. Empty means auto-detect.
param.benchmark.n_gpu_layers.name: GPU layers
param.benchmark.n_gpu_layers.desc: Number of model layers offloaded to GPU. 0 means CPU-only; 99 means as many as possible. Multiple values are supported for testing.
param.benchmark.n_cpu_moe.name: CPU MOE
param.benchmark.n_cpu_moe.desc: Force MOE (Mixture-of-Experts) expert computation to run on CPU. 0 disables.
param.benchmark.split_mode.name: Split mode
param.benchmark.split_mode.desc: How to split the model across multiple GPUs. none = no split, layer = split by layer, row = split by row.
param.benchmark.no_kv_offload.name: Disable KV offload
param.benchmark.no_kv_offload.desc: Disable offloading the KV cache to GPU and force the KV cache to be stored in CPU memory.
param.benchmark.flash_attn.name: Flash Attention
param.benchmark.flash_attn.desc: Enable Flash Attention optimization to speed up attention computation, especially with long contexts.
param.benchmark.mmap.name: Memory-mapped loading
param.benchmark.mmap.desc: When disabled (0), load the model without memory mapping (mmap) and read weights into memory normally. mmap usually reduces copying at startup and lets the OS page data on demand, resulting in faster startup and better memory behavior. Disabling mmap can avoid mapping failures, SIGBUS, or stalls in some file systems / network drives / permission-limited / container environments, but often increases one-time memory usage and load time. Toggle this option when loading is slow, fails, or is unstable to find a more stable/faster combination.
param.benchmark.tensor_split.name: Tensor split
param.benchmark.tensor_split.desc: Tensor split ratio across multiple GPUs. Example: 0 means auto; 0.5/0.5 splits evenly across two GPUs.
param.benchmark.direct_io.name: Direct I/O
param.benchmark.direct_io.desc: Enable Direct I/O, which affects model loading speed. Enabled by default. llama.cpp's implementation may be problematic; if you encounter loading issues, disable it.
page.benchmark_v2.title: Benchmark V2
page.benchmark_v2.status.token_sum_limit: Prompt tokens + max output tokens must not exceed context length ({ctx})
